resharedPost.text:
It is particularly meaningful to me to be co-organizing an event focused on helping beginners in tech with Honza Javorek (aka Javor), because he was one of my mentors when I was starting out in the industry.

When I joined Apiary in 2017 I was—to quote one of my colleagues—a complete “greenhorn”. My wonderful manager Ladislav Prskavec was gradually exposing me to all areas related to site reliability engineering and web development. This inevitably led to testing and I was pointed towards “Javor” to get solid fundamentals and practice with Mocha and Sinon (yup, those were the days). What followed went far beyond just learning about mocking, stubbing and injecting dependencies—I got to observe our Chief Community Gardener at work. From working with volunteers, to writing educational & respectful code reviews (with best memes, of course), creating documentation to improve teams and organizations, I’ve tried to learn all I could from Honza. It was also incidentally thanks to Javor that I got to help out with PyConCZ in 2018, learn new skills and make new friends in the community. 

Working in the same company turned to volunteering in the same community, and with time turned into friendship as I watched one of my favorite mentors leave the corporate world and follow his passion for helping other greenhorns in tech by creating Junior Guru. It was quite an experience watching as he threw himself into the new adventure, testing various business models, bootstrapping revenue and never losing sight of the main goal—helping juniors find meaningful new careers in IT.

With Honza’s support PyConCZ 2023 introduced the Beginners’ Track for the first time and it was a massive success. So it wasn’t exactly a surprise when Mia Bajić mentioned to me that EuroPython 2025 is considering adding a program focused on beginners and has approached Honza regarding a possible cooperation. If you’ve ever met Mia then you know she’s very effective at recruiting people—she let me simmer on the idea of working with Honza again a bit, and ultimately I took her up on the offer of joining the wonderful community of EuroPython volunteers.

Together with Honza we’ve been cooking the Beginners’ Day Unconference for you all for a while. With the expertise of our international guest panelists Barbora Němcová, Gábor Ambrózy, Jana Moudrá, Manivannan Selvaraj, Michaela Trčková, Miloslav Ježek and Šimon Kořený we’ll be discussing a wide range of topics related to early-stage careers in tech. Landing the job, getting over the initial challenges, and ultimately thriving and growing, we’ll cover it all.

With Honza we’re looking forward to meeting you all at the EuroPython Beginners’ Day Unconference in Prague in just a few days.

https://lnkd.in/dmikMywF

comments.text: So excited to be part of this incredible journey with you and Honza! Can’t wait to meet everyone at the Beginners’ Day Unconference in Prague 🙌 🚀

comments.text:
I don't know what to say! This is so lovely.

I'll just add that the learning goes both ways and you were always among the people I found inspiring, be it because your dedication and drive or communication and organization skills. You showed me that being a greenhorn doesn't have to mean being lost, and that even a greenhorn can add extreme amounts of collateral value.

I'm looking forward to hosting the Beginners' Day Unconference with you!
